en.extract:
The Solomons white-eye or New Georgia white-eye (Zosterops kulambangrae) is a species of bird in the family Zosteropidae. It is endemic to the New Georgia Islands in the Solomon Islands. It is also known as Zosterops rendovae but this name properly refers to the grey-throated white-eye.
The species is widespread in the New Georgia Group, occurring on Kolombangara, Vonavona, Kohinggo, New Georgia, Vangunu and Nggatokae. It regularly visits small islets.
It is 12 cm long and mainly green on its upper body and olive-yellow on the underside. It has a narrow white ring around the eye, blackish forehead and lores, a black bill and yellowish legs.

The '''Solomons white-eye''' or '''New Georgia white-eye''' ('''''Zosterops kulambangrae''''') is a species of [[bird]] in the family [[Zosteropidae]]. It is [[Endemism|endemic]] to the [[New Georgia Islands]] in the [[Solomon Islands]]. It is also known as ''Zosterops rendovae'' but this name properly refers to the [[grey-throated white-eye]].<ref name=IBC>{{cite web|author=Internet Bird Collection|url=http://ibc.lynxeds.com/species/solomon-white-eye-zosterops-kulambangrae|title=Solomon White-eye (Zosterops kulambangrae)|access-date=9 January 2010}}</ref><ref>{{cite journal|author=Mees, G. F.|year=1955|url=http://www.repository.naturalis.nl/document/150019|title=The name of the white-eye from Rendova Island (Solomon Islands)|journal=Zoologische Mededelingen|volume=33|issue=28|pages=99–100}}</ref>

The species is widespread in the New Georgia Group, occurring on [[Kolombangara]], [[Vonavona]], [[Kohinggo]], [[New Georgia]], [[Vangunu]] and [[Nggatokae]]. It regularly visits small islets.<ref>{{cite book|author1=Mayr, Ernst  |author2=Jared M. Diamond |name-list-style=amp |year=2001|title=The Birds of Northern Melanesia: Speciation, Ecology and Biogeography|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=AUUQsAuk9qsC&q=%22zosterops+rendovae%22&pg=PA492|publisher=Oxford University Press | isbn=978-0-19-514170-2}}</ref>

It is 12 cm long and mainly green on its upper body and olive-yellow on the underside. It has a narrow white ring around the eye, blackish forehead and lores, a black bill and yellowish legs.<ref>{{cite book|author1=Doughty, Chris |author2=Nicolas Day |author3=Andrew Plant  |name-list-style=amp |year=1999|title=Birds of the Solomons, Vanuatu & New Caledonia|publisher=Christopher Helm|location=London}}</ref>
